message:
test(connectors): add happy-path E2E suites for amplitude/qq + capability-rejection roundtrip

Adds three new test suites that exercise the real connector code paths
against wiremock-served HTTP fixtures, mirroring the suite-style harness
already in use for other connectors in the workspace.

New tests:
- connectors/amplitude/tests/connector_suite_happy_path.rs (271 lines)
  Drives AmplitudeConnector through handshake -> simulate -> invoke for
  the cohorts.list operation, asserting capability-token gating, idem-
  potency-class metadata, and response shape.
- connectors/qq/tests/connector_suite_happy_path.rs (~150 lines)
  Same shape but for the QQ connector.
- crates/fcp-e2e/tests/capability_rejection_roundtrip_e2e.rs (331 lines)
  Cross-connector test covering capability-rejection roundtrips, ensuring
  invocations without the required capability fail closed and that the
  decision receipt round-trips cleanly through the host.

Cargo manifest changes pull in the dev-deps the new tests need:
- connectors/amplitude/Cargo.toml: add chrono, fcp-crypto, fcp-e2e
- connectors/qq/Cargo.toml: add ciborium, fcp-e2e

Cargo.lock updated to reflect the new dev-dep edges in the resolution
graph (no new external crate versions, only path-dep wiring).

No production code changes in this commit.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -38,6 +38,8 @@ hex.workspace = true
 
 [dev-dependencies]
 chrono.workspace = true
+ciborium.workspace = true
+fcp-e2e = { path = "../../crates/fcp-e2e" }
 fcp-crypto.workspace = true
 fcp-testkit.workspace = true
 wiremock.workspace = true
